clinical_study.brief_title: Extended Olaparib Maintenance (3 Years vs. 2 Years) in BRCA-Mutated Advanced Ovarian Cancer After Complete Response (EXTEND-OLA)
clinical_study.official_title: A Phase 2 Randomized Trial of Extended Olaparib Maintenance Therapy for 3 Years Versus Standard 2-Year Olaparib Maintenance in Patients With BRCA1 or BRCA2-Mutated Advanced High-Grade Serous Ovarian, Fallopian Tube, or Primary Peritoneal Carcinoma Who Achieved Complete Response to First-Line Platinum-Based Chemotherapy

brief_summary.textblock: This trial evaluates whether extending olaparib maintenance therapy to 3 years (versus the current standard 2-year duration) reduces the risk of disease recurrence in patients with BRCA1 or BRCA2-mutated advanced high-grade serous ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma who achieved complete clinical response following first-line platinum-taxane chemotherapy and cytoreductive surgery.

detailed_description.textblock: Based on the SOLO-1 trial, olaparib maintenance therapy for 2 years after complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy in BRCA-mutated advanced ovarian cancer is standard of care, with a 5-year PFS rate of approximately 48% versus 21% in placebo. However, late recurrences (after olaparib discontinuation) remain common, suggesting that extending the duration of maintenance may further delay or prevent relapse. Patients who achieve a complete radiographic and CA-125 response after surgery and chemotherapy represent a particularly favorable subgroup in whom durable remission may be achievable. This study randomizes BRCA1/2-mutated advanced ovarian cancer patients who have completed 2 years of standard olaparib maintenance and remain in complete response (CR) to continue olaparib 300 mg BID for an additional year (total 3 years) versus discontinue olaparib (standard 2-year duration). Patients must have CA-125 within normal limits and no evidence of radiographic disease at the time of randomization. Stratification: BRCA1 vs BRCA2, stage IIIC vs IV, HRD score (Myriad MyChoice >= 42 vs < 42 among non-BRCA HRR carriers; for BRCA-mutated patients, HRD is assumed positive), and surgical debulking status (upfront R0 vs interval debulking).

primary_outcome.measure: Progression-Free Survival 2 (PFS2): time from randomization to second progression or death
primary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until second disease progression or death, assessed up to 60 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Time to First Recurrence (TTFR) after randomization
secondary_outcome.time_frame: From randomization until first CA-125 progression (GCIG criteria) or radiographic recurrence, up to 60 months
From randomization until death from any cause, up to 84 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Health-Related Quality of Life (HRQoL) assessed by FOSI and EORTC QLQ-OV28
secondary_outcome.time_frame: Every 6 months during treatment and follow-up, up to 36 months

secondary_outcome.measure: Safety and tolerability (NCI CTCAE v5.0), with focus on hematologic toxicity and MDS/AML occurrence
secondary_outcome.time_frame: During treatment period, up to 12 months; long-term follow-up for MDS/AML up to 84 months

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria: - Histologically confirmed FIGO Stage III or IV high-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC), high-grade endometrioid carcinoma, or undifferentiated ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma - Deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation confirmed by validated assay (germline testing preferred; somatic testing on tumor tissue acceptable) - Homologous Recombination Deficiency (HRD) positive status: for BRCA1/2 carriers, HRD positivity is assumed; Myriad MyChoice HRD score is collected as exploratory biomarker but not required - Completed primary treatment: cytoreductive surgery (upfront or interval) + at least 6 cycles of first-line platinum-taxane chemotherapy (carboplatin/paclitaxel or carboplatin/paclitaxel/bevacizumab); bevacizumab use in frontline is allowed but must have been discontinued prior to or at the start of olaparib maintenance - Currently on olaparib maintenance therapy and approaching completion of 2 years (18-24 months elapsed); patients must be within 6 months of completing their 2-year olaparib course - Complete clinical response at time of randomization: (a) CA-125 within institutional normal limits (< 35 U/mL) AND (b) No evidence of disease on CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast or MRI abdomen/pelvis within 8 weeks of randomization - No radiographic evidence of recurrence since initiation of olaparib maintenance - ECOG Performance Status 0-1 - Age >= 18 years - Adequate bone marrow at time of randomization: ANC >= 1500/mm3, platelets >= 100,000/mm3, hemoglobin >= 10 g/dL (without transfusion support in prior 4 weeks) - Adequate hepatic function: total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN - Adequate renal function: creatinine clearance >= 51 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault) - Willingness to continue olaparib and to comply with study visits and procedures - Signed informed consent
Exclusion Criteria: - Evidence of disease recurrence (CA-125 progression per GCIG criteria or radiographic progression) at any point during initial olaparib maintenance prior to randomization - Low-grade serous carcinoma, mucinous carcinoma, clear cell carcinoma, or other non-HGSC histologies - Non-BRCA HRR mutations (ATM, PALB2, RAD51C/D, BRIP1) without BRCA1/2 mutation; these patients may be eligible for separate HRD cohort - Prior PARP inhibitor other than olaparib (e.g., niraparib, rucaparib, talazoparib) as part of first-line maintenance; patients who previously participated in a PARP inhibitor trial in the upfront setting are excluded unless that agent was olaparib - Evidence of MDS or acute myeloid leukemia (AML) at time of enrollment; complete blood count abnormalities suspicious for MDS require bone marrow biopsy for confirmation before enrollment - Active second malignancy requiring systemic treatment (excluding non-melanoma skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ) - Known hypersensitivity to olaparib - Significant gastrointestinal disorder that may affect olaparib absorption - Pregnancy or breastfeeding; effective contraception required for women of childbearing potential (though most patients will be post-menopausal due to disease and treatment) - Anticipated inability to comply with olaparib dosing schedule or study visits

intervention.description: Olaparib 300 mg orally twice daily for an additional 12 months (total maintenance duration approximately 3 years), following completion of the initial 2-year standard olaparib maintenance period. Dose reductions to 250 mg BID and then 200 mg BID are permitted for toxicity management per protocol.

Discontinuation of olaparib after 2 years of standard maintenance (control arm). Patients are observed with CA-125 measurements every 3 months and CT imaging every 6 months. Olaparib placebo (blinded) will be administered to maintain blinding per protocol.
